channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Companion Cube case for Steam Machine is canceled by Valve after Dbrand bafflingly forgot to get permission to make the thing
item.description: 'You had two jobs! Ask Valve, and make the thing': The Companion Cube case for the Steam Machine is no more.

item.title: Fitbit’s Gemini AI coach is giving users ‘unhinged’ fitness advice — here’s why users are saying they ‘cannot wait for my trial to end’
item.description: Users say Fitbit’s Gemini AI is giving them highly questionable fitness advice.

item.title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music but isn’t banning it outright
item.description: Tidal shared its new policies regarding AI-generated music today and how the platform plans to "protect artists" and "inform listeners." Instead of banning it outright, starting on July 15th Tidal will label tracks it has identified as being 100 percent AI-generated with an icon. But starting today those tracks will no longer be monetizable. "Tidal's […]...

item.title: Forget Ryzen AI! AMD launches its SLOWEST processor in years, because 'not all customers can afford a new PC' — 2019 Zen+ CPU will at least be compatible with Windows 11
item.description: AMD's latest move seems to be two steps backward for budget laptops in a bid to cater to price-sensitive consumers

item.title: En vidéo : pourquoi le Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide est le modèle le plus important pour Samsung
item.description: Et si le salut des smartphones pliants passait par un tout nouveau format ? Avec les rumeurs autour d'un mystérieux Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide plus compact, Samsung prépare en coulisses une riposte stratégique face au futur iPhone pliable d'Apple.

item.title: Accusés d’avoir organisé la pénurie de RAM, Samsung et SK Hynix annoncent 590 milliards pour doubler la production
item.description: Quatre jours après la plainte qui les accuse d'avoir étranglé l'offre de mémoire, Samsung et SK Hynix annoncent environ 590 milliards de dollars pour doubler leur production. Le geste est spectaculaire, son effet sur le prix de votre RAM se fera attendre des années.

item.title: Tesla déploie enfin le FSD V14 Lite : qu’apporte-t-il vraiment et pourquoi l’Europe reste sur la touche
item.description: 4 millions de voitures Tesla reçoivent enfin une mise à jour substantielle de leur logiciel de conduite autonome supervisée. Une belle avancée, mais qui ne règle pas le fond du problème pour les clients qui, finalement, n'auront jamais ce pour quoi ils ont déboursé plusieurs milliers d'euros. Explications.

item.title: Sony’s next-gen PlayStation will go ‘beyond the living room’
item.description: Sony hinted in a recent Q&A with investors that the next generation PlayStation will offer some kind of experience that lets you play games outside of your living room. Here's the relevant portion from the transcript, emphasis mine: Q: How can you bring back to the PlayStation platform users who migrated to gaming PCs during […]

item.title: Fin d’une époque : Canal+ va fermer toutes ses boutiques physiques au 1er septembre 2026
item.description: Canal+ ferme son réseau de boutiques agréées au 1er septembre 2026. Une page de l'histoire de la chaîne se tourne, quarante ans après son lancement.

item.title: Layoffs looming, Xbox union members argue for transparency and good-faith bargaining
item.description: Xbox union members argue for transparency and good-faith bargaining.

item.title: Watch out, Amazon: the Kobo eReader now has a Goodreads rival
item.description: Kobo users can now automatically sync their reading progress to StoryGraph, making it easier to track books, reading stats, and challenges without relying on Amazon’s Goodreads.

item.title: Google Gemini débarque dans les Renault : ce qui change à bord (et pourquoi c’est mieux qu’Android Auto)
item.description: Renault déploie progressivement Gemini sur les véhicules équipés de son système multimédia openR link. L'assistant IA de Google succède à Google Assistant, mais qu'est-ce que ça va changer concrètement pour les possesseurs de R4, R5, Mégane et autres Scénic électriques ?

item.title: Ces 2 planètes géantes à 1 110 années-lumière sont moins denses que la barbe à papa : les astronomes cherchent l’explication
item.description: Ce 28 juin 2026, les astronomes ont confirmé l'existence de 8 228 exoplanètes dans la Voie lactée. Certaines sont étranges, comme deux géantes gazeuses qui sont actuellement sur le devant de la scène.

item.title: Top tech of the month: the best new gadgets we've tested for July 2026
item.description: From a tiny-but-mighty handheld fan to a speaker that delivers 'thunderous power', here are the gadgets that impressed us most this month.

item.title: US bans Polestar but not Volvo in baffling EV ruling — data security fears force exit for premium brand, while its sister company gets a green light
item.description: New US rule will ban Polestar EVs over data security fears, even though none of the EVs it sells in North America are built in China.

item.title: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
item.description: OpenAI is releasing some sort of device related to its AI-powered coding tool, Codex, on July 15th. In a video posted to X on Monday, OpenAI shows a square-shaped device with several buttons, alongside the caption, "Your favorite Codex shortcuts are getting an upgrade." This isn't the mysterious AI-powered device OpenAI is working on with […]

item.title: The US Supreme Court restricts use of geofence warrants
item.description: The US Supreme Court has surprisingly decided to restrict geofence warrants.

item.title: Waymo and Uber quietly part ways in Phoenix
item.description: The companies confirmed to TechCrunch that their unusual partnership in Phoenix recently ended after nearly three years..

item.title: WhatsApp permet le nom d’utilisateur à la place du numéro de téléphone
item.description: La messagerie WhatsApp, propriété du groupe Meta, va prochainement permettre à ses utilisateurs de communiquer sans partager leur numéro de téléphone, en introduisant lors d’une prochaine mise à jour l’usage de noms, a annoncé l’entreprise lundi.

item.title: Here's why you shouldn't plug a power strip into a smart plug
item.description: It may be tempting to control an entire power strip with a smart plug, but there are some things you should know about this setup.

item.title: The most underrated business discipline is hospitality
item.description: How a hotel candle reminds this CEO that AI is important, but so is being human in the business environment. I don’t remember the valuation of the deal. I don’t remember most of the presentations. I don’t even remember many of the people who were in the room. But more than 20 years later, I still have a candle from the hotel.

item.title: FBI warns of Russian Intelligence phishing campaign abusing Signal support services to target VIPs and high-value government and military targets — this is how to secure your account
item.description: Russian Intelligence are trying to hijack Signal accounts by tricking users into sending their Backup Recovery Keys

item.title: Who funds the future? The capital balance that fuels innovation
item.description: What comes next depends on how funding aligns and who steps up to build it. For most of the past century, the United States has not left innovation to chance.

item.title: MacBook Ultra rumors: What to expect including the touchscreen, chips, release date and more
item.description: Apple's rumored touchscreen MacBook may launch soon, here's what to expect.

item.title: 1 Go de RAM coûte aussi cher qu’en 2010
item.description: Le prix du gigaoctet de RAM est revenu à son niveau de 2010. La crise actuelle de la mémoire efface 16 ans de baisses.

item.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
item.description: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

item.title: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘RAMageddon’
item.description: The world's two largest memory chip companies vow to build more memory lab fabs as South Korea positions itself as an AI tech powerhouse country.
